headline: Court interpreter shortage affects 4,300 non-Malay cases
context: Shortage of court interpreters in Malaysia delayed approximately 4,300 cases involving non-Malay and non-English speaking parties in 2024.

cards.big: 4,300 court cases delayed due to shortage of interpreters in 2024
cards.sub: Tamil, Mandarin, and indigenous language speakers most affected

cards.big: Courts of Judicature Act requires proceedings in Malay with interpretation available
cards.sub: Only 120 certified court interpreters serve entire national court system

cards.big: Article 5 right to fair trial requires parties to understand proceedings fully
cards.sub: Language barriers effectively deny access to justice for non-Malay speakers

cards.big: Orang Asli defendants in 87 cases had no interpreter for their mother tongue
cards.sub: Indigenous communities face compounded disadvantage in legal proceedings

cards.big: Justice you cannot understand in your own language is justice denied
Chief Registrar approved hiring of 45 additional interpreters for 2025
